skills.name: plan-feature
skills.description: Turn a rough idea into a concrete, implementation-ready spec with acceptance criteria, test matrix, and ADR stub.

skills.name: claude-md-audit
skills.description: Audit a CLAUDE.md for loading-discipline (ADR-0081): classify each block Layer 0/1/2/delete and propose a routing plan that shrinks the always-loaded footprint without @import theater.

skills.name: claude-md-rollout
skills.description: Advance the ADR-0081 CLAUDE.md loading-discipline rollout by one repo: pick next untouched, run /claude-md-audit --apply, open a PR, update the tracker. Driven by /schedule or run at /frame-standup.

skills.name: spec-review
skills.description: Fact-check a plan or spec produced by another agent against domain knowledge and actual code. Finds factual errors, gaps, and contradictions before implementation starts.

skills.name: scaffold
skills.description: Generate types, skeleton implementations, and test stubs from a spec. Follows /plan-feature.

skills.name: investigate
skills.description: Evidence-first root-cause analysis. No code edits. Produces symptom, evidence, cause map, candidates.

skills.name: validate
skills.description: Pre-merge quality gate: spec coverage, TypeScript safety, auth, logging, tests, framework-specific checks.

skills.name: deploy
skills.description: Release orchestration: pre-flight checklist, blast radius, rollback plan, monitoring targets.
triggers: what do I need to ship

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Generate runbook / handoff doc: overview, how to run, debug guide, operations, open items.

skills.name: hardening
skills.description: Security/resilience/observability gap analysis. No fixes — audit and ranked finding list.

skills.name: doc-refactor
skills.description: Normalize README, docs/, inline comments, CLAUDE.md. Generate Mermaid diagrams.

skills.name: test-expand
skills.description: Identify untested branches and propose new tests. Never modifies implementation code.

skills.name: sweep
skills.description: Maintenance pass: stale TODOs, unused imports, commented-out code, console.*, config duplication.

skills.name: techdebt
skills.description: Meta-skill: scan for debt, propose patches, apply to allowed paths. Improves all other skills.

skills.name: setup-ci-cd
skills.description: One-shot CI/CD: pre-commit hooks, GitHub Actions workflows, security scans, coverage gates.

skills.name: push-all
skills.description: Safe commit: scan for secrets, warn on protected branches, draft smart message, commit.

skills.name: observe
skills.description: Production observability triage: logs, metrics, traces, error reports. Severity + action plan.

skills.name: recon
skills.description: Dense codebase reconnaissance: structure, entry points, stack, architecture, data flows.

skills.name: summarize
skills.description: Summarize a file, module, or pasted text. --style=brief (default) or --style=detailed.

skills.name: roadmap
skills.description: Analyze codebase and produce short/medium/long-term roadmap with priorities and effort.

skills.name: scaffold-app
skills.description: Scaffold a brand-new application from canonical template: langgraph-app, browser-extension, python-scraper.

skills.name: frame-standup
skills.description: Sync all tracked repos, audit the latest daily-logger post for accuracy, produce a prioritized day plan, and present interactive options that expand to full subagent prompts via framework skills.
triggers: what should I work on today
sync repos and plan the day
check the daily-logger and plan work

skills.name: gastown
skills.description: Gas Town alignment agent. Four modes: audit (adoption progress), plan (sprint plan), pilot (GasTownPilot UI), sync (update GitHub roadmap issues).

skills.name: workbench
skills.description: Start/stop/inspect the 6-tile tmux multi-repo development workbench.

skills.name: agent-debug
skills.description: LangGraph / multi-agent debugging: graph map, failure trace, diagnosis. No code changes.

skills.name: pr-review
skills.description: Structured PR audit: correctness, LangGraph invariants, auth, logging, test coverage.

skills.name: screenshot-audit
skills.description: Classify screenshot pairs as regression/enhancement/intentional/false-positive. Structured audit report.

skills.name: rag-audit
skills.description: Audit RAG pipeline: chunking, embeddings, vector store, retriever config, seeding.

skills.name: extension-audit
skills.description: Browser extension audit: bundle size, permissions, CSP, prompt injection, message passing.

skills.name: council-review
skills.description: Multi-persona expert council review of any draft doc. Each persona critiques independently; synthesizes final version.

skills.name: skill-loader
skills.description: Enumerate available skills, fuzzy-match to purpose, output install/remove commands.

skills.name: skill-create
skills.description: Turn a reusable workflow or session pattern into a convention-compliant skill directory. Creates files on disk, registers in skill-catalog.json.
triggers: save this workflow as a skill

skills.name: daily-logger
skills.description: Daily-logger architecture briefing: 4-phase pipeline, persona format, council pattern, invariants.

skills.name: lint-audit
skills.description: Run @frame/eslint-plugin rules + artifact scanner, cross-reference with TECHDEBT.md, produce structured quality report.
triggers: what lint issues do we have

skills.name: resume-audit
skills.description: Structured audit of resume vs. job description with adversarial gap probing, gap classification (True/Framing/Adjacent), and tuning knobs for improvement.
triggers: how well does my resume match

skills.name: adr
skills.description: Create, list, search, or update Architecture Decision Records in decisions/adr/.

skills.name: init
skills.description: Session initializer — load environment context, check services, show active sessions, suggest skills. Run at the start of any session for full situational awareness.

skills.name: grill-with-docs
skills.description: Socratic alignment before planning. Updates CONTEXT.md and stages ADR drafts in-loop. No code. Output: shared design concept, CONTEXT.md diff, ADR stub drafts.
triggers: I'm not sure what we're building

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Red-green-refactor loop. Writes failing test first, verifies red, makes minimal change to green, offers refactor. Guidance — does not block edits.

skills.name: deepen
skills.description: Find shallow modules and propose deepening refactors. Ousterhout depth metric (interface simplicity × implementation richness). No edits.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Apply consistent severity/effort/domain labels and prioritize backlog. Output: label set + ordered backlog. Optional --apply writes labels via gh.

skills.name: skill-metrics
skills.description: Compute adoption metrics from skill-telemetry.jsonl + suggestion-telemetry.jsonl. Invocations, sequencing, suggestion-followed rate, ADR target gaps. Honest numbers only.
triggers: are we using the new skills

skills.name: vault
skills.description: Maintain the selfco LLM Wiki (~/selfco) — a Karpathy-style Obsidian vault: append-only raw/ source layer + LLM-owned wiki/ of source/entity/concept/synthesis pages + index.md + log.md, with ~/selfco/CLAUDE.md as the schema. Modes: init (scaffold + write schema + Obsidian plugins + repo entity stubs), ingest <path|url> (land a source → summary page → update entity/concept pages → index → log), research <topic> (ingest but fetch the sources), query <question> (answer from the wiki w/ citations, file explorations back), lint (health-check), sync [--since=7d] (fold the ojfbot activity feed into repo entity pages), handoff (fold the current session into the vault — summary + full text of every artifact), orient, note <title>. There's also a tool-agnostic consumer-app Agent Skill (core/.claude/skills/vault/consumer/SKILL.md) covering ingest/query/note/orient/handoff for claude.ai web / iPhone / Claude Desktop via the GitHub connector on ojfbot/selfco or a local mcp-obsidian server, and a copy-paste export prompt ~/selfco/prompts/session-handoff.md for foreign chat agents (Dia, claude.ai) — see knowledge/connectors.md, ADR-0085/0070. Distinct from /daily-logger (chronological blog) and /bead (per-repo handoff).
triggers: what does my wiki say about
export this session to my vault
wrap up this session into selfco

skills.name: prototype
skills.description: Build throwaway code that answers one question: an interactive terminal harness for logic/state-machine edge cases, or N radically different UI variants switchable by URL param. Record the verdict, then delete.
triggers: build a scratch version to test X

skills.name: caveman
skills.description: Ultra-compressed communication mode — drops articles, hedging, preamble, recap; keeps full technical accuracy, paths, commands, code. Stays on until the user says 'normal mode'.

skills.name: zoom-out
skills.description: In-loop orientation for code you're already working in: walk up the call graph and module boundaries — who calls this, what it depends on, blast radius if changed. No report file; for a full cold-start repo overview use /recon.
triggers: how does this fit into the system

skills.name: writing-fragments
skills.description: Interview the user to extract varied raw material for an article (stories, claims, examples, definitions, objections); consolidate into a fragments file. Step 1 of the writing pipeline (then /writing-beats, then /writing-shape).
triggers: help me brain-dump for an article

skills.name: writing-beats
skills.description: Structure an article as an ordered sequence of beats (reader-journey decision points), then develop one beat at a time with sign-off. Step 2 of the writing pipeline; takes a fragments file, hands beats to /writing-shape.
triggers: outline this article as a journey
what's the beat sheet for this piece

skills.name: writing-shape
skills.description: Turn rough or beat-drafted markdown into finished article prose, deciding the form (prose/list/table/callout/code/heading) of every paragraph rather than defaulting to walls of text or bullet soup. Step 3 of the writing pipeline.

skills.name: git-guardrails
skills.description: State the dangerous-git policy and audit or wire the repo's guardrails — .claude/settings.json deny/ask rules (via /update-config) and an optional pre-push hook. Read-only by default; only edits settings when asked.

skills.name: gated-slice
skills.description: Decompose a large effort into Control-Gated Slices (ADR-0086): independently-shippable vertical slices, each delivered through ordered Control Gates with Entrance + Success Criteria expressed as TPMs (MOE → MOP → TPM), with any action-taking enforcement control required to mature through a Brassboard/shadow (observe-only) stage before going Operational, promoted by a data-gated RIDM decision. Plan only, no code. Distinct from /plan-feature (single-feature spec→test-matrix) and /orchestrate (execution).
triggers: how do I roll out this gate

skills.name: bead
skills.description: Inter-session continuity via beads — small dated markdown files in a .handoff/ ledger that let any future session (chat or Code) reconstruct context. Distinct from /handoff (post-ship runbook docs).
triggers: pick up where we left off
what was the last session about
write a brief for claude code

skills.name: resume
skills.description: Hardened, evidence-tiered session pickup — reconstructs a provenance ledger from [git]/[PR] (ground truth) + [DOLT]/[READ] beads (self-report), tagging every claim VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED/CONFLICT/GAP so an agent cannot confabulate completed work. Preflight STOPs on untrustworthy ground; --verify backfills git-grounded report beads for work that shipped with no self-report. Distinct from /bead orient (cheap markdown glance) and /handoff (post-ship runbooks).
triggers: pick up where we left off
what did the last session actually do

skills.name: diagram-intake
skills.description: Read a hand-drawn priority diagram (photo), map goals to canonical repos, cross-reference roadmap + open blockers, output structured per-app priorities compatible with /frame-standup Step 7.

skills.name: frame-dev
skills.description: Start, stop, or check status of all Frame OS dev servers. Displays a clickable URL guide on completion.

skills.name: orchestrate
skills.description: 4-layer progressive decomposition pipeline: takes per-app priorities from /frame-standup or /diagram-intake, decomposes into tasks via planning agents, then executes via worktree-isolated implementation agents. Context narrowing at each layer.

skills.name: scaffold-frame-app
skills.description: Scaffold a new Frame OS client application with full architecture patterns pre-wired: 4-package monorepo, Module Federation remote, Carbon tabbed dashboard, side panel, shell integration, and deterministic build+test validation (28-item checklist).

skills.name: skill-audit
skills.description: Audit the skill library against the Anthropic skill-architecture patterns (nine categories + authoring tips): coverage gaps, straddlers, drift, missing Gotchas. Deterministic signals from a script; judgment from an LLM pass. Read-only — reports, never edits skills.
triggers: which skill categories am I missing

skills.name: selfco-ingest
skills.description: Stage a URL or chat session into the selfco vault via the Notion inbox (chat-side only — the local promoter lands files on disk). Discuss the source, structure a wiki/sources/ draft, write to inbox with suggested wikilinks.

skills.name: adopt-stack
skills.description: Decide how to integrate a mature external stack into an opinionated one: a library-vs-application gate, then per-opinion wrap/absorb/reject calls. Output: a measurement table + a recorded decision table. Read-only — proposes integration shape, writes no production code.

skills.name: speculative-pass
skills.description: Generate a fleet of speculative research sub-agents pointed at one subsystem, each entering from an oblique angle yet forced to land on something buildable on the real substrate. Emits n disjoint stances (native domain / forced landing / anti-pattern / seed prompt), optionally runs k inline, and writes a handoff for the rest. The harness is the asset; the subsystem is disposable.

skills.name: day-run
skills.description: Dispatch the day's ready roadmap slices: compile file-canonical roadmaps onto the queue, run the Gate-0 day-runner (isolated worktrees, branch+PR+movement-proposal at each slice boundary), then the human merge ritual records movement.

skills.name: fleet-onboard
skills.description: Register a new (or drifted) repo on every fleet enumeration surface — northstar registry, daily-logger sweep, cockpit fleet cards, core ecosystem table, frame-standup lists, install-agents — with a reconcile mode that diffs all surfaces against the northstar registry.
